title: bridge-slack: Slack timestamp overflow when system clock is before Unix epoch
description: In bridge-slack/src/slack.rs verify_slack_signature(), if SystemTime::now().duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH) fails (system clock before epoch), now defaults to 0 via unwrap_or(0). The subsequent check 'ts > now + 60 || now - ts > 300' then evaluates 'now - ts' as an i64 subtraction. If ts is a positive integer (normal case), 0 - ts will be negative and the condition '0 - ts > 300' is false, so the timestamp is accepted regardless of how old it is. This bypasses the replay-attack guard entirely when the system clock is broken. The native transport.rs correctly uses .context() to fail explicitly on broken clocks; the Slack bridge should too.
